freshnessTargets: Shift to an outdoor or semi-outdoor location cluster (e.g., park bench, street corner, bus stop)
Introduce an object family not recently used (e.g., natural_item, clothing, tech_item)
Use a presence mode that shows more of the character or environment (e.g., full_presence, environmental_portrait)
avoidMotifs: Covered walkways and bookstore aisles (shelter_pause, bookish_pause clusters)
Opening captions about weather or studying fatigue
narrativeNudges: Frame the moment as a small, spontaneous encounter rather than a planned ritual
Let the emotional landing be a quiet observation without forward-looking promise
Connect the object to a fleeting memory or a subtle shift in perception
captionAdvice: Start with a concrete, immediate sensory detail (touch, sound, light) not weather-based
Use a short, reflective observation as the ending, avoiding 'gentle order' or 'shy attachment' closure
Incorporate one slightly longer, flowing sentence amidst shorter ones for rhythm
imageAdvice: Prioritize a wider shot that shows the character in relation to a new environment
If a close-up, focus on an action-in-progress (e.g., adjusting, holding, noticing) rather than a static object
Use natural, available light to create a different mood (e.g., soft dusk, artificial streetlight)
summary: Shift from indoor, ritualistic pauses to a more open, observational moment that retains intimacy but feels spontaneously discovered.
